chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَلْبَسُ الْمُحْرِمُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهُ لِبَاسُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 83 – What the one in Ihraam can wear, from the clothes, and what is disliked for him, his clothing

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/278)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/278)
text: أَحْرَمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فِي ثَوْبَيْ كُرْسُفٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from one of our companions,
(It has been narrated) from one of themasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww wore Ihraam in two cotton cloths’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from certain persons of our people who has said the following: “One of ’A’immah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, assumed the state of Ihram in two pieces of cotton fabric.’”
